zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnese di Massa di Cagliari於大約1185年出生在義大利薩德尼亞卡格利亞利時，她的父親Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari27歲，母親Adelisa Malaspina21歲。她和Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於大約1257年在她的家鄉享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnese di Massa di Cagliari于大约1185年出生在意大利薩德尼亞卡格利亞利时，她的父亲Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari27岁，母亲Adelisa Malaspina21岁。她和Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres至少有1个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于大约1257年在她的家乡享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Massa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Massey
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).

variants.name: Massaro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Pasquale, Rocco, Antonio, Gennaro, Vito, Carlo, Gaetano, Nunzio, Antoninette.

Italian: occupational or status name from massaro, which in northern Italy denoted a tenant farmer or share cropper and in central and southern Italy could also denote an agent or steward of a rural estate, from medieval Latin massarius, a derivative of massa ‘farm, estate’. Compare Massa and Massara .

nameMeanings.name: Agnese
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Agnes
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnese di Massa di Cagliari was born about 1185, in Cagliari, Sardinia, Italy, her father, Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari, was 27 and her mother, Adelisa Malaspina, was 21. She had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres. She died about 1257, in her hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnese di Massa di Cagliari est née environ 1185, à Cagliari, Sardegna, Italie, son père, Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Adelisa Malaspina, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles avec Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres. Elle est décédée environ 1257, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnese di Massa di Cagliari um 1185 in Cagliari, Sardinien, Italien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari 27 und ihre Mutter Adelisa Malaspina 21. Sie hatte mit Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1257 im Alter von 74 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnese di Massa di Cagliari è nata il circa 1185 nel Cagliari, Sardegna, Italia, sua padre, Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Adelisa Malaspina, aveva 21 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie con Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres. Morì circa 1257, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnese di Massa di Cagliariが約1185年にイタリアサルデグナカグリアリで生まれた時，父Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliariは27歳，母Adelisa Malaspinaは21歳であった。彼女はMariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torresとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1257年に故郷で74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnese di Massa di Cagliari(이)가 대략 1185년 이탈리아 사르데그나 카그리아리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelisa Malaspina(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1257년 그녀의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnese di Massa di Cagliari nasceu aproximadamente 1185, em Cagliari, Sardegna, Itália, seu pai, Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Adelisa Malaspina, tinha 21 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas com Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1257, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnese di Massa di Cagliari родилась около 1185 г. в местности кагляри, сардегна, Италия, её отцу по имени Guglielmo I Salusio IV di Massa, 7th Judge of Cagliari было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelisa Malaspina, 21. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери от Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres. Она умерла около 1257 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas con Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres. Murió aproximadamente 1257, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años.
